project_summary:
This proposal outlines the development of a comprehensive Task Management System designed to enhance team productivity through real-time collaboration, secure task tracking, and intuitive user management. Over a 12-week period, we will deliver a robust platform built on your existing technology stack (Node.js, React, PostgreSQL, AWS), enabling users to create, organize, and share tasks while maintaining strict security and performance standards. The solution focuses on high-priority features suc

**Project Metrics:**
- Modules: User Management, Task Management, Collaboration, Dashboard & Reporting
- Total Tasks: 44
- Total Effort: 125 days
- Story Points: 173
- Timeline: 12 weeks
- Architecture: Modular Monolith
- Identified Risks: 4
**Tech Stack:** Backend: Express.js | Frontend: React | Database: PostgreSQL

tasks.title: Design Auth UI Flows
tasks.description: Create wireframes and high-fidelity mockups for Registration, Login, Password Reset, and Profile screens ensuring WCAG 2.1 AA compliance.
acceptance_criteria: All auth and profile screens approved by PO with WCAG 2.1 AA audit passed.
tasks.technical_notes: Ensure focus states and color contrast meet AA standards; include error state designs for invalid inputs.

tasks.title: Setup DB Schema for Users
tasks.description: Design and implement PostgreSQL tables for Users, including fields for email, password_hash, name, avatar_url, preferences (JSONB), and role.
acceptance_criteria: Migration script runs successfully; schema matches architecture document; unique constraint on email enforced.
tasks.technical_notes: Use UUID for primary keys; ensure password_hash column is NOT NULL; index email for fast lookups.

test_cases.test_name: Happy path: User schema migration runs successfully and creates expected tables with unique email constraint
Edge case: Re-running migration is idempotent and does not alter schema
Failure scenario: Inserting duplicate email violates unique constraint

tasks.title: Implement Registration API
tasks.description: Build POST /api/v1/auth/register endpoint with email/password validation, bcrypt hashing, and user creation logic.
acceptance_criteria: Valid registration creates user with hashed password; invalid inputs return 400; duplicate email returns 409.
tasks.technical_notes: Use bcrypt with cost factor 10; validate email format and password strength (min 8 chars, 1 number, 1 special char).

tasks.title: Implement Login & JWT Auth
tasks.description: Build POST /api/v1/auth/login endpoint to verify credentials, generate JWT access/refresh tokens, and set HttpOnly cookies.
acceptance_criteria: Valid credentials return JWT; invalid credentials return 401; tokens stored in HttpOnly cookies.
tasks.technical_notes: Implement rate limiting (5/min); ensure JWT expiration is short (15m) with refresh token rotation.

tasks.title: Implement Password Reset Flow
tasks.description: Create POST /api/v1/auth/forgot-password to generate secure reset tokens and trigger SendGrid email with reset link.
acceptance_criteria: Email sent with valid reset link; token expires after defined duration; password update endpoint works.
tasks.technical_notes: Store reset token hash in DB; ensure token is single-use; integrate SendGrid API for email delivery.

tasks.title: Build Auth UI Components
tasks.description: Develop React/TypeScript components for Registration, Login, and Password Reset forms with client-side validation.
acceptance_criteria: Forms submit correctly; validation errors display immediately; accessible keyboard navigation works.
tasks.technical_notes: Use React Hook Form for validation; ensure ARIA labels on all inputs; handle loading and error states gracefully.

tasks.title: Implement Profile Management API
tasks.description: Build GET/PUT /api/v1/users/profile endpoints to retrieve and update user details, including avatar upload logic.
acceptance_criteria: Profile data saved to DB; avatar uploaded to S3 and URL stored; preferences persist as JSONB.
tasks.technical_notes: Generate pre-signed S3 URLs for direct upload; validate image types (jpg, png) and size limits.

tasks.title: Build Profile UI & Avatar Upload
tasks.description: Create Profile page with form for name/preferences and drag-and-drop avatar upload component.
acceptance_criteria: User can update name and preferences; avatar uploads and displays correctly; changes persist on refresh.
tasks.technical_notes: Use AWS SDK for pre-signed URL generation on frontend; implement image preview before upload.

tasks.title: Implement Admin User Management API
tasks.description: Build GET /api/v1/admin/users and PUT /api/v1/admin/users/:id endpoints with RBAC middleware for Admin role verification.
acceptance_criteria: Admin can list all users; Admin can modify user status/role; non-admins receive 403 Forbidden.
tasks.technical_notes: Implement RBAC middleware checking 'Admin' role; assume fixed role set per ASM-003; paginate user list.

tasks.title: Build Admin User Management UI
tasks.description: Develop Admin dashboard table to view users, edit roles/status, and handle pagination and search.
acceptance_criteria: Admin sees user list; can edit roles/status; changes reflect immediately; accessible table structure.
tasks.technical_notes: Use server-side pagination; implement role dropdown based on ASM-003 assumptions; add confirmation modals for edits.

tasks.title: Security & Auth Unit Tests
tasks.description: Write comprehensive unit and integration tests for all auth endpoints, password hashing, and JWT validation logic.
acceptance_criteria: 100% code coverage for auth module; all negative test cases (invalid creds, expired tokens) pass.
tasks.technical_notes: Test bcrypt hashing strength; verify JWT signature validation; test rate limiting behavior.

tasks.title: DevOps Auth Pipeline Setup
tasks.description: Configure GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for automated testing, security scanning, and deployment of Auth module.
acceptance_criteria: Pipeline runs on PR; security scan passes; deployment to staging environment successful.
tasks.technical_notes: Include SAST/DAST scans; configure environment variables for JWT secrets and SendGrid keys securely.

tasks.title: Design Task CRUD UI Components
tasks.description: Create React/TypeScript components for task creation, editing, and deletion forms with validation states and responsive layout.
acceptance_criteria: UI mockups for task forms approved with WCAG 2.1 AA contrast and focus states verified.
tasks.technical_notes: Ensure form fields map to Task entity attributes: title, description, due_date, priority, labels.

tasks.title: Implement Task Database Schema
tasks.description: Define PostgreSQL tables for Task, Subtask, and ActivityLog with UUIDs, soft delete flags, and JSONB for labels/preferences.
acceptance_criteria: Migration scripts run successfully; schema matches data model with correct foreign keys and indexes.
tasks.technical_notes: Add tsvector column for full-text search on title/description. Ensure is_deleted boolean defaults to false.

test_cases.test_name: Happy path: Task schema migration runs successfully and creates tables with correct foreign keys and indexes
Edge case: Foreign key constraint prevents orphaned task creation
Failure scenario: Deleting a user that has associated tasks is restricted

tasks.title: Build Task CRUD API Endpoints
tasks.description: Develop Node.js/Express endpoints for creating, reading, updating, and soft-deleting tasks with ownership validation.
acceptance_criteria: API returns 200/201/404/403 correctly; soft delete sets is_deleted flag without removing row.
tasks.technical_notes: Implement middleware to check task ownership before update/delete. Use transaction for audit log insertion.

tasks.title: Implement Subtask Logic
tasks.description: Create API logic and DB relationships to link subtasks to parent tasks and track individual completion status.
acceptance_criteria: Subtasks can be created under a parent; parent status updates based on subtask completion logic.
tasks.technical_notes: Ensure cascade delete for subtasks if parent is deleted (hard delete) or soft delete logic propagates.

tasks.title: Develop Task Filtering & Search API
tasks.description: Implement PostgreSQL full-text search and filtering logic for status, priority, date range, and labels.
acceptance_criteria: Search query returns results in <200ms; filters correctly narrow result set based on params.
tasks.technical_notes: Use pg-tsvector for full-text search. Optimize indexes on status, priority, and created_at columns.

tasks.title: Build Task List & Detail Views
tasks.description: Develop React components to display task lists with filtering controls and detailed task view with edit/delete actions.
acceptance_criteria: Task list renders data from API; filters update list dynamically; edit modal opens with pre-filled data.
tasks.technical_notes: Implement optimistic UI updates for filtering. Ensure loading states and error handling are present.

tasks.title: Implement Task Completion Flow
tasks.description: Create UI and API logic to mark tasks as complete with optional notes and update timestamps.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking complete updates status to 'Done'; completion note is saved and displayed.
tasks.technical_notes: Trigger activity log entry upon completion. Ensure subtask completion logic updates parent if applicable.

tasks.title: Implement Subtask UI Components
tasks.description: Build React components for adding, editing, and checking off subtasks within the parent task view.
acceptance_criteria: Subtasks list renders under parent; checkbox toggles completion; new subtask form works.
tasks.technical_notes: Use nested list structure. Ensure parent progress bar updates visually when subtasks are checked.

tasks.title: Write Unit & Integration Tests
tasks.description: Create Jest/Supertest suites for Task CRUD, subtask logic, and search performance benchmarks.
acceptance_criteria: 100% code coverage for core logic; search performance test passes <200ms threshold.
tasks.technical_notes: Include negative tests for invalid inputs and unauthorized access attempts.

tasks.title: Accessibility Audit & Fixes
tasks.description: Conduct manual and automated WCAG 2.1 AA audit on task forms and lists; fix contrast, ARIA labels, and keyboard nav.
acceptance_criteria: Automated audit tool (e.g., axe) reports zero critical issues; manual keyboard navigation works.
tasks.technical_notes: Focus on form validation error announcements and focus management in modals.

tasks.title: Document Task API & Schema
tasks.description: Generate Swagger/OpenAPI documentation for all Task Management endpoints and database schema diagrams.
acceptance_criteria: Swagger UI renders all endpoints with correct request/response examples; schema diagram is up to date.
tasks.technical_notes: Include error codes and rate limit info in documentation.

tasks.title: Deploy Task Module to Staging
tasks.description: Configure CI/CD pipeline to deploy Task Management module to AWS staging environment with database migrations.
acceptance_criteria: Staging environment accessible; database migrations applied automatically; health check passes.
tasks.technical_notes: Ensure environment variables for DB connection and S3 are correctly injected.

tasks.title: Design Task Sharing UI Flow
tasks.description: Create wireframes and high-fidelity mockups for the task sharing modal, email invite input, and confirmation states.
acceptance_criteria: Design files approved by PM with WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratios verified.
tasks.technical_notes: Ensure design accounts for existing user validation state as per ASM-001.

tasks.title: Implement Task Share API Endpoint
tasks.description: Develop POST /api/v1/tasks/:id/share endpoint to validate recipient email, check existing user status, and create share record.
acceptance_criteria: API returns 200 on valid invite to existing user; 404 if user not found; share record created in DB.
tasks.technical_notes: Enforce ASM-001: Reject invites for non-existent users. Use JWT for auth.

tasks.title: Integrate SendGrid for Invites
tasks.description: Configure SendGrid template and implement logic to send email invites with secure tokens upon share creation.
acceptance_criteria: Email received by recipient with valid link; token expires after defined duration.
tasks.technical_notes: Use existing SendGrid integration (INT-001). Handle rate limiting.

tasks.title: Build Task Sharing Frontend Component
tasks.description: Implement React component for sharing tasks, including email input validation and success/error feedback.
acceptance_criteria: Component renders correctly; validates email format; displays success message on API 200.
tasks.technical_notes: Connect to POST /api/v1/tasks/:id/share. Ensure keyboard navigation support.

tasks.title: Design Comment Thread UI
tasks.description: Create UI designs for threaded comments, including nesting, timestamps, and user avatars.
acceptance_criteria: Design approved with clear visual hierarchy for nested comments.
tasks.technical_notes: Focus on readability for deep nesting levels.

tasks.title: Implement Comment CRUD API
tasks.description: Develop endpoints for creating, updating, and deleting threaded comments with parent_comment_id support.
acceptance_criteria: Comments saved with correct parent linkage; soft delete supported; audit log updated.
tasks.technical_notes: Ensure self-referencing foreign key in Comment table.

tasks.title: Build Comment Thread Frontend
tasks.description: Implement React component for displaying and submitting threaded comments with real-time updates.
acceptance_criteria: Comments render in correct thread order; new comments appear instantly; edit/delete functions work.
tasks.technical_notes: Integrate WebSocket listener for real-time updates.

tasks.title: Setup WebSocket Notification Service
tasks.description: Configure Socket.io server to handle real-time events for comments and task assignments.
acceptance_criteria: Server accepts connections; emits events to specific user rooms; handles disconnects gracefully.
tasks.technical_notes: Ensure scalability for 1000 concurrent users (NFR-001).

tasks.title: Implement Real-time Notification UI
tasks.description: Build in-app notification bell and toast system to display real-time updates for comments and assignments.
acceptance_criteria: Notifications appear instantly upon event; unread count updates; clickable links navigate to task.
tasks.technical_notes: Use WebSocket client to subscribe to user-specific channels.

tasks.title: Design Team Board Interface
tasks.description: Create designs for team creation, member management, and team-level task board view.
acceptance_criteria: Designs cover team creation flow and board layout; approved by PM.
tasks.technical_notes: Assume flat team structure per GAP-004 constraints.

tasks.title: Implement Team Management API
tasks.description: Develop endpoints for creating teams, adding members, and assigning tasks to team boards.
acceptance_criteria: Teams created with unique IDs; members added successfully; tasks assignable to team context.
tasks.technical_notes: Implement Many-to-Many relationship between User and Team.

tasks.title: QA Test Collaboration Features
tasks.description: Execute test cases for sharing, comments, notifications, and team management including edge cases.
acceptance_criteria: All critical test cases passed; zero critical bugs; performance under 200ms for shared task queries.
tasks.technical_notes: Verify ASM-001 assumption (existing users only) is enforced in tests.

tasks.title: Design Dashboard Layout & Widgets
tasks.description: Create high-fidelity UI mockups for the dashboard including task summary widgets, overdue counts, and activity feed, ensuring WCAG 2.1 AA compliance.
acceptance_criteria: Design mockups approved by Product Owner and pass automated accessibility contrast checks.
tasks.technical_notes: Ensure layout is responsive; define color palette for status indicators (overdue, complete, etc.).

tasks.title: Implement Dashboard Data Aggregation API
tasks.description: Develop backend endpoints to aggregate task counts by status, priority, and due date for the current user, optimizing SQL queries for performance.
acceptance_criteria: API returns aggregated data in <200ms for 95% of requests under load.
tasks.technical_notes: Use PostgreSQL aggregation functions; implement caching strategy if query complexity increases.

tasks.title: Build Dashboard Widget Components
tasks.description: Implement React components for displaying task summary widgets (counts, overdue, recent activity) using TypeScript and the design system.
acceptance_criteria: Widgets render accurate data from API and meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards for screen readers.
tasks.technical_notes: Use React Query for data fetching; ensure loading states and error handling are implemented.

tasks.title: Integrate Real-time Activity Feed
tasks.description: Connect the dashboard activity feed to the WebSocket service to display real-time updates on shared task changes.
acceptance_criteria: Activity feed updates instantly upon task changes without page refresh.
tasks.technical_notes: Subscribe to specific WebSocket channels based on user's shared tasks.

tasks.title: Optimize Dashboard Query Performance
tasks.description: Profile and optimize database queries used for dashboard widgets to ensure they meet the 200ms response time NFR under concurrent load.
acceptance_criteria: Query execution time remains under 200ms with 1000 concurrent users in load test.
tasks.technical_notes: Add necessary indexes on status, due_date, and created_at columns; review execution plans.

test_cases.test_name: Happy path: Dashboard query executes under 200ms with realistic data volume
Edge case: Dashboard query remains under 200ms with empty tables
Failure scenario: Query exceeds 200ms without performance indexes; adding indexes brings it under limit

tasks.title: Execute Accessibility Audit on Dashboard
tasks.description: Perform manual and automated testing of the dashboard interface to ensure full compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA standards.
acceptance_criteria: Zero critical or major accessibility violations found in automated and manual audit.
tasks.technical_notes: Test with screen readers (NVDA/VoiceOver) and keyboard navigation only.

tasks.title: Write Dashboard Integration Tests
tasks.description: Create unit and integration tests for dashboard API endpoints and frontend widget components to ensure data accuracy and stability.
acceptance_criteria: Test coverage >80% for dashboard modules; all tests pass in CI pipeline.
tasks.technical_notes: Mock external dependencies; test edge cases like empty task lists.

tasks.title: Document Dashboard API & Usage
tasks.description: Generate API documentation for dashboard endpoints and create user guide sections for interpreting dashboard widgets.
acceptance_criteria: Swagger/OpenAPI docs updated; user guide section published and reviewed.
tasks.technical_notes: Include example responses and error codes for all dashboard endpoints.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users must register with email and password
Users must login with email/password
Users must reset forgotten passwords via email
User profiles support name, avatar, preferences
Admin users can manage all users and roles
Users create tasks with title, description, due date, priority, labels
Users edit their own tasks
Users delete their own tasks
Users mark tasks as complete with completion notes
Users filter tasks by status, priority, date range, label
Users search tasks by title/description with full-text search
Tasks support subtasks with completion tracking
Users can set recurring tasks (weekly, monthly)
Users share tasks with other users via email invite
Users comment on shared tasks with threading
Users receive real-time notifications for assignments and comments
Users can create teams and team-level task boards
Activity feed shows recent changes to shared tasks
Users see dashboard with task summary widgets
Users generate task completion reports by date range

non_functional_requirements.requirement: API response under 200ms for 95% of requests
Passwords hashed with bcrypt, sessions with JWT
HTTPS encryption for all data in transit
Support for Firefox, Chrome, Safari, Edge

gaps.description: The BRD states 'Users share tasks with other users via email invite' but does not specify if the recipient must already be a registered user or if an account is created automatically upon acceptance.
gaps.impact: May lead to incorrect implementation of the invitation flow and user onboarding logic.
gaps.suggested_clarification: Should the email invite system create a new user account automatically if the recipient is not registered, or only allow invites to existing users?

gaps.description: The BRD mentions 'Admin users can manage all users and roles' but does not define the specific roles available (e.g., Admin, Member, Guest) or the specific permissions associated with each role.
gaps.impact: Development of the role-based access control (RBAC) system cannot be completed without defined role hierarchies and permissions.
gaps.suggested_clarification: What are the specific user roles defined in the system and what permissions does each role possess?

gaps.description: The requirement 'Users receive real-time notifications' does not specify the delivery channels (e.g., in-app only, email, push notifications, SMS) beyond the SendGrid integration for email.
gaps.impact: The real-time notification architecture (e.g., WebSocket implementation) may be over- or under-engineered based on missing channel requirements.
gaps.suggested_clarification: Which specific channels must be supported for real-time notifications (e.g., in-app, email, push)?

gaps.description: The BRD specifies 'Users can create teams' but does not define the limits on team size, the number of teams a user can join, or the hierarchy of teams (e.g., sub-teams).
gaps.impact: Database schema design for team relationships and validation logic for team creation may be incomplete.
gaps.suggested_clarification: Are there any limits on the number of teams a user can create or join, and is there a hierarchy for teams?

gaps.description: The BRD requires 'full-text search' for tasks but does not specify the search engine technology (e.g., PostgreSQL full-text search, Elasticsearch) or the scope of the search (e.g., across all tasks or only user's tasks).
gaps.impact: Performance and scalability of the search feature may be compromised if the wrong technology is chosen or if the scope is misunderstood.
gaps.suggested_clarification: Which technology should be used for full-text search, and should users search only their own tasks or all accessible tasks?

in_scope.description: Implement email and password registration flow with validation and account creation.
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: User can successfully register with valid email/password; invalid inputs are rejected; account created in DB.

in_scope.description: Implement email/password authentication with JWT session generation.
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Valid credentials return JWT; invalid credentials return 401; session persists across requests.

in_scope.description: Implement forgot password flow triggering email via SendGrid with secure reset token.
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Email sent with reset link; token valid for defined duration; password updated successfully.

in_scope.description: Allow users to update name, upload avatar (S3), and set preferences.
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Profile data saved to DB; avatar stored in S3 and retrievable; preferences persist.

in_scope.description: Admin interface to view, edit, and manage user roles (RBAC implementation pending role definition).
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Admin can view user list; Admin can modify user status/role (subject to role definition clarification).

in_scope.description: Create tasks with title, description, due date, priority, and labels.
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Task saved with all fields; validation enforced; task visible in user's list.

in_scope.description: Allow users to update their own task details.
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Changes saved; audit log updated; UI reflects changes immediately.

in_scope.description: Allow users to delete their own tasks (soft delete preferred for audit).
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Task removed from active view; data retained in DB for history.

in_scope.description: Mark tasks as complete with optional completion notes.
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Status updated to 'Complete'; completion note saved; timestamp recorded.

in_scope.description: Filter task list by status, priority, date range, and label.
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: List updates dynamically based on selected filters; query performance <200ms.

in_scope.description: Search tasks by title/description using PostgreSQL full-text search.
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Search returns relevant results; performance meets NFR-002.

in_scope.description: Create subtasks under parent tasks with individual completion tracking.
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Subtasks linked to parent; parent completion logic reflects subtask status.

in_scope.description: Share tasks with other users via email invite (logic pending GAP-001 clarification).
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Invite email sent via SendGrid; recipient can access task upon acceptance.

in_scope.description: Threaded commenting system on shared tasks.
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Comments displayed in thread order; real-time updates visible to sharers.

in_scope.description: In-app notifications for assignments and comments (email via SendGrid for critical alerts).
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Notification appears instantly on UI; email sent for critical events.

in_scope.description: Create teams and assign team-level task boards (limits pending GAP-004 clarification).
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Team created; board visible to team members; tasks assignable to team.

in_scope.description: Display recent changes to shared tasks in an activity feed.
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Feed updates in real-time; shows user, action, and timestamp.

in_scope.description: Dashboard displaying task summary widgets (counts, overdue, etc.).
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Widgets render accurate data; load time <200ms.

in_scope.description: System architecture supporting 1000 concurrent users.
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Load test passes with 1000 concurrent users; error rate <1%.

in_scope.description: Password hashing (bcrypt) and JWT session management.
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Passwords stored as hashes; JWT validation enforced on all protected routes.

in_scope.description: UI implementation meeting WCAG 2.1 AA standards.
in_scope.acceptance_criteria: Automated and manual audit passes WCAG 2.1 AA criteria.

out_of_scope.reason: FR-013 is marked as Low Priority and the 12-week timeline with 5 developers requires focusing on High/Medium priority features to meet the launch deadline.
FR-020 is marked as Low Priority; dashboard widgets (FR-019) provide sufficient summary data for the MVP launch.
BRD specifies SendGrid for email and does not explicitly mandate mobile push notification services (e.g., Firebase/APNS) within the current scope or budget.
BRD only specifies SendGrid (Email) and does not include SMS gateway integration requirements.
GAP-004 indicates missing requirements for team hierarchy; scope limited to flat team creation until clarified.

assumptions.assumption: Email invites (FR-014) will only be sent to users who already have an account in the system.
assumptions.impact_if_wrong: If auto-registration is required, the user onboarding flow and database schema will need significant rework, delaying Phase 3.

assumptions.assumption: PostgreSQL full-text search is sufficient to meet NFR-002 (200ms response) for the expected data volume.
assumptions.impact_if_wrong: If performance degrades, a dedicated search engine (Elasticsearch) may be required, increasing cost and complexity.

assumptions.assumption: The 'Admin' role (FR-005) will have a fixed set of permissions defined before Phase 1 development concludes.
assumptions.impact_if_wrong: RBAC implementation will be delayed or require refactoring if roles are undefined.

assumptions.assumption: AWS credits will cover the infrastructure costs for the 12-week development and testing period.
assumptions.impact_if_wrong: Budget overrun requiring immediate cost-cutting measures or additional funding.

constraints.impact: Low priority features (FR-013, FR-020) must be excluded to ensure on-time delivery of High/Medium priority items.

constraints.constraint: Technology Stack: Node.js/Express, React/TypeScript, PostgreSQL, AWS.
constraints.impact: No flexibility to introduce new languages or databases; all solutions must fit within this stack.
Limits ability to hire external contractors or purchase premium third-party services beyond existing contracts.

constraints.constraint: NFR-002: API response under 200ms for 95% of requests.
constraints.impact: Database queries and API design must be highly optimized; complex joins or unoptimized search may violate this constraint.

client_signoff_questions.question: For FR-014 (Task Sharing), should the email invite system automatically create a new user account if the recipient is not registered, or restrict sharing to existing users only?
client_signoff_questions.why_important: This determines the complexity of the invitation flow and user onboarding logic (GAP-001).

client_signoff_questions.question: What are the specific user roles (e.g., Admin, Member, Guest) and their associated permissions for FR-005 (Admin User Management)?
client_signoff_questions.why_important: Without defined roles, the RBAC system cannot be fully implemented (GAP-002).

client_signoff_questions.question: For FR-016 (Real-time Notifications), are in-app notifications sufficient, or are email/push notifications required for all events?
client_signoff_questions.why_important: This impacts the notification architecture and integration scope (GAP-003).

client_signoff_questions.question: Are there any limits on the number of teams a user can create or join, and is a team hierarchy required for FR-017?
client_signoff_questions.why_important: This affects database schema design and validation logic (GAP-004).

client_signoff_questions.question: For FR-011 (Full-Text Search), should users search only their own tasks or all tasks they have access to via sharing?
client_signoff_questions.why_important: This defines the search scope and performance requirements (GAP-005).

proposal.executive_summary: This proposal outlines the development of a comprehensive Task Management System designed to enhance team productivity through real-time collaboration, secure task tracking, and intuitive user management. Over a 12-week period, we will deliver a robust platform built on your existing technology stack (Node.js, React, PostgreSQL, AWS), enabling users to create, organize, and share tasks while maintaining strict security and performance standards. The solution focuses on high-priority features such as user authentication, core task lifecycle management, and team collaboration tools, ensuring a market-ready product within the defined budget of $150,000.

objectives.objective: Enable secure and seamless user onboarding and management
objectives.success_metric: 100% of users can register, login, and reset passwords without assistance; 99.9% uptime for authentication services.

objectives.objective: Streamline task creation, tracking, and completion workflows
objectives.success_metric: 95% of beta testers can create and complete tasks without assistance; API response times under 200ms for 95% of requests.

objectives.objective: Facilitate real-time team collaboration and visibility
objectives.success_metric: Real-time notifications and comments function with zero latency for shared tasks; 100% of shared tasks support threaded discussions.

objectives.objective: Ensure system scalability and security compliance
objectives.success_metric: System supports 1000 concurrent users; 100% compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards; zero critical security vulnerabilities at launch.

deliverables.deliverable: User Authentication and Profile Management Module
deliverables.acceptance_criteria: Users can register, login, and reset passwords via email; profiles support avatars stored in S3; Admin interface allows user management; passwords hashed with bcrypt and sessions managed via JWT.
Users can create, edit, delete, and complete tasks with subtasks; filtering and full-text search (PostgreSQL) return results under 200ms; soft delete implemented for audit trails.

Tasks can be shared via email invite (to existing users); threaded comments function in real-time; in-app notifications trigger instantly for assignments and comments; team boards are created and accessible.
Dashboard widgets display accurate task summaries; UI passes WCAG 2.1 AA audit; application is fully functional across Firefox, Chrome, Safari, and Edge.
Load testing confirms support for 1000 concurrent users with <1% error rate; Sentry error monitoring integrated; 99.9% uptime achieved during stress testing.

phases.name: Setup & User Management
milestones: User Registration and Login Flow Complete

phases.name: Core Task CRUD
milestones: Task Creation and Completion Logic Live

phases.name: Collaboration Features
milestones: Task Sharing and Email Invites Functional

responsibilities: Implement PostgreSQL database schema and queries
Ensure security (bcrypt, JWT) and performance optimization
Support database optimization for search and filtering
Implement responsive design and accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA)
Integrate real-time WebSocket connections for collaboration
Develop dashboard widgets and task views
Implement task management forms and filtering logic
Ensure cross-browser compatibility (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
Perform load testing for 1000 concurrent users

risks.risk: Ambiguity in Task Sharing logic (GAP-001) regarding whether email invites create new accounts or require existing users.
risks.mitigation: Immediate clarification required from the Product Owner before Phase 3 development begins; current scope assumes invites are for existing users only.

risks.risk: Undefined User Roles and Permissions (GAP-002) may delay the implementation of the Admin User Management feature.
risks.mitigation: Define specific roles (e.g., Admin, Member) and permissions in the first week to ensure RBAC implementation aligns with Phase 1 deliverables.

risks.risk: PostgreSQL full-text search performance may not meet the 200ms response time requirement (NFR-002) under high load.
risks.mitigation: Conduct early performance prototyping in Phase 2; if benchmarks are missed, optimize queries or consider indexing strategies immediately.

risks.risk: Scope creep due to undefined team hierarchy limits (GAP-004) potentially expanding the complexity of the Team Management feature.
risks.mitigation: Strictly limit initial implementation to flat team creation as per current assumptions; defer hierarchy features to Phase 2 pending clarification.

next_steps: Schedule a kickoff meeting to resolve GAP-001 (Task Sharing logic) and GAP-002 (User Roles definition).
Confirm AWS infrastructure setup and SendGrid API key availability for the development environment.
Finalize the detailed project schedule and assign specific tasks to the 5-person development team.

architecture.justification: Given the 12-week timeline, 5-person team, and $150k budget, a Modular Monolith is the optimal choice. It avoids the operational overhead and complexity of microservices while enforcing clear boundaries between User, Task, and Collaboration modules. This pattern allows the team to move fast, share a single database (PostgreSQL) for transactional integrity (critical for task assignments and comments), and deploy a single artifact, meeting the NFR-003 uptime and NFR-002 latency requirements without over-engineering.

components.name: API Gateway & Auth Service
components.responsibility: Handles all incoming HTTP requests, JWT validation, rate limiting, and routing to internal modules. Manages User Registration, Login, and Password Reset flows.

components.name: User Profile Module
components.responsibility: Manages user profile data, avatar uploads to S3, and preference storage. Includes Admin user management logic.

components.name: Task Core Module
components.responsibility: Handles CRUD operations for tasks, subtasks, filtering, and full-text search. Enforces ownership and soft-delete logic.

components.name: Collaboration Module
components.responsibility: Manages task sharing, team creation, threaded comments, and activity feed generation. Handles email invites via SendGrid.

components.name: Dashboard & Reporting Module
components.responsibility: Aggregates task data to generate summary widgets and activity feeds for the user dashboard.

components.name: Notification Service
components.responsibility: Processes real-time events (comments, assignments) and pushes updates via WebSocket and triggers email alerts via SendGrid.

backend.justification: Mandated by BRD constraints and team expertise. Express provides the flexibility to build a modular monolith quickly within the 12-week timeline.

frontend.justification: Mandated by BRD constraints. TypeScript ensures type safety for complex task data structures, and React is the company standard.

database.justification: Mandated by BRD. PostgreSQL is chosen for its robust relational integrity (critical for task relationships), JSONB support for flexible preferences, and built-in full-text search capabilities (tsvector) to meet NFR-002 without external search engines.

key_services: EC2 (or Elastic Beanstalk for simplified deployment)
infrastructure.justification: Mandated by BRD. Using existing AWS credits and services minimizes setup time. RDS ensures high availability (NFR-003) and managed backups.

apis.description: Register a new user with email and password.
Update user profile and upload avatar.
Create a new task with title, description, due date, priority, labels.
List tasks with filtering (status, priority, date, label) and search.
Mark task as complete with optional note.
Create a subtask under a parent task.
Share task with another user via email invite.
Add a threaded comment to a task.
List teams the user belongs to.
Get recent activity feed for shared tasks.

security.authentication: JWT (JSON Web Tokens) with short-lived access tokens and refresh tokens stored in HttpOnly cookies.
security.authorization: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) middleware checking user roles (Admin/Member) and task ownership before allowing operations.
security.data_encryption: Passwords hashed with bcrypt (NFR-004). All data in transit encrypted via HTTPS (NFR-005). S3 buckets configured for private access with pre-signed URLs for avatar uploads.

needs_further_design.reason: Ambiguity exists regarding whether email invites create new accounts or require existing users (GAP-001). This impacts the database schema for invitations and the user onboarding flow.
Specific roles and permissions for Admin vs. Member are not defined (GAP-002), preventing finalization of the RBAC middleware logic.
It is unclear if full-text search should be limited to the user's own tasks or include all shared tasks (GAP-005), which affects query complexity and performance optimization.

sow.project_overview: This Statement of Work defines the engagement for the development of a comprehensive Task Management System. The project aims to deliver a secure, scalable, and collaborative productivity platform enabling teams to create, organize, and track tasks with real-time features. The solution will be built using the client's mandated technology stack (Node.js, React, PostgreSQL, AWS) over a 12-week period, adhering to a fixed budget of $150,000. The system will support user authentication, core task lifecycle management, team collaboration via sharing and comments, and dashboard reporting, ensuring compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards and supporting 1000 concurrent users.

objectives.objective: Enable secure and seamless user onboarding and management
objectives.success_criteria: 100% of users can register, login, and reset passwords without assistance; 99.9% uptime for authentication services.

objectives.objective: Streamline task creation, tracking, and completion workflows
objectives.success_criteria: 95% of beta testers can create and complete tasks without assistance; API response times under 200ms for 95% of requests.

objectives.objective: Facilitate real-time team collaboration and visibility
objectives.success_criteria: Real-time notifications and comments function with zero latency for shared tasks; 100% of shared tasks support threaded discussions.

objectives.objective: Ensure system scalability and security compliance
objectives.success_criteria: System supports 1000 concurrent users; 100% compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards; zero critical security vulnerabilities at launch.

in_scope: User Registration, Login, and Password Reset with JWT/Bcrypt security
User Profile Management including Avatar upload to S3
Admin User Management interface (RBAC pending role definition)
Task Creation, Editing, Deletion (Soft Delete), and Completion with notes
Task Filtering and Full-Text Search using PostgreSQL
Task Sharing via Email Invite (to existing users only)
Real-time In-App Notifications for assignments and comments
Team Creation and Team-Level Task Boards
Activity Feed for shared task changes
Integration with SendGrid, AWS S3, and Sentry
out_of_scope: Creation of new user accounts via email invite (Assumption: invites only for existing users)
assumptions: Email invites (FR-014) will only be sent to users who already have an account in the system.
PostgreSQL full-text search is sufficient to meet NFR-002 (200ms response) for the expected data volume.
The 'Admin' role (FR-005) will have a fixed set of permissions defined before Phase 1 development concludes.
AWS credits will cover the infrastructure costs for the 12-week development and testing period.

deliverables.deliverable: User Authentication and Profile Management Module
deliverables.description: Implementation of secure registration, login, password reset, and profile management including avatar upload and admin user controls.
deliverables.acceptance_criteria: Users can register/login/reset password; profiles support avatars in S3; Admin interface allows user management; passwords hashed with bcrypt; sessions managed via JWT.

deliverables.description: Full CRUD functionality for tasks including subtasks, filtering, and full-text search with soft delete capabilities.
deliverables.acceptance_criteria: Users can create/edit/delete/complete tasks; filtering and search return results under 200ms; soft delete implemented; subtasks track completion status.

deliverables.description: Task sharing via email, threaded comments, real-time notifications, team boards, and activity feeds.
deliverables.acceptance_criteria: Tasks shared via email invite to existing users; threaded comments function in real-time; in-app notifications trigger instantly; team boards created and accessible.

deliverables.description: Dashboard widgets for task summaries and full UI compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA standards across supported browsers.
deliverables.acceptance_criteria: Dashboard widgets display accurate data; UI passes WCAG 2.1 AA audit; application functional on Firefox, Chrome, Safari, and Edge.

deliverables.description: Load testing, error monitoring integration, and final deployment preparation.
deliverables.acceptance_criteria: Load test confirms 1000 concurrent users with <1% error rate; Sentry integrated; 99.9% uptime achieved during stress testing.

milestones.acceptance_criteria: All user auth flows tested; security audit passed; Admin interface functional.
Task CRUD operations verified; search performance <200ms; subtask logic validated.
Task sharing and comments functional; real-time notifications working; team boards accessible.
Dashboard widgets rendering; WCAG 2.1 AA audit passed; cross-browser compatibility verified.
1000 concurrent user load test passed; Sentry monitoring active; production deployment completed.

responsibilities: Build task management forms and filtering logic
Perform load testing for 1000 concurrent users

communication.status_reports: Weekly written status report every Friday
communication.meetings: Bi-weekly sprint review and planning meetings

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Ambiguity in Task Sharing logic regarding account creation for invitees.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Immediate clarification required from Product Owner; scope assumes invites for existing users only.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Undefined User Roles and Permissions may delay Admin feature implementation.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Define specific roles and permissions in the first week.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: PostgreSQL full-text search performance may not meet 200ms requirement under high load.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Conduct early performance prototyping; optimize queries or indexing immediately if benchmarks missed.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Scope creep due to undefined team hierarchy limits.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Strictly limit initial implementation to flat team creation; defer hierarchy features to Phase 2.

sow.change_management: Any changes to the approved scope matrix, timeline, or budget must be submitted in writing via a Change Request Form. The Vendor will assess the impact on cost and schedule and provide a formal Change Order for Client approval before work commences. Unapproved changes will not be included in the deliverables.
sow.acceptance_process: Upon completion of each milestone, the Vendor will submit the deliverables for review. The Client has five (5) business days to test and provide written acceptance or a detailed list of defects. If no written feedback is received within this period, the deliverable is deemed accepted. Defects must be resolved by the Vendor at no additional cost before the next milestone payment is released.

terms_and_conditions.intellectual_property: Upon full payment of all fees, the Client shall own all rights, title, and interest in the custom deliverables created specifically for this project. The Vendor retains ownership of pre-existing code libraries, frameworks, and tools used in the development.
terms_and_conditions.confidentiality: Both parties agree to keep all proprietary information, including business requirements, technical designs, and user data, strictly confidential. This obligation survives the termination of this agreement for a period of three (3) years.
terms_and_conditions.warranty: The Vendor warrants that the deliverables will perform substantially in accordance with the specifications for a period of ninety (90) days following the Acceptance Date. The Vendor will repair or replace any non-conforming deliverables at no additional cost during this period.
terms_and_conditions.dispute_resolution: In the event of a dispute, the parties agree to first attempt to resolve the issue through good-faith negotiation. If unresolved, the dispute shall be settled by binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association, located in the jurisdiction of the Vendor's principal place of business.

test_cases.test_name: Happy path: User schema migration runs successfully and creates expected tables with unique email constraint
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running and accessible
Migration script for users is present at ./migrations/001_users.sql
steps: Run migration script: psql -h localhost -U testuser -d testdb -f ./migrations/001_users.sql
Connect to database and describe users table: \d users
Check for unique constraint on email column: SELECT conname FROM pg_constraint WHERE conrelid = 'users'::regclass AND contype = 'u';
test_cases.expected_result: Migration completes with exit code 0; users table includes columns id (serial PK), email (varchar), password_hash (varchar), created_at timestamp, updated_at timestamp; unique constraint named users_email_key exists on email column
test_cases.pass_criteria: Migration exit code = 0 AND column list matches expected AND constraint name 'users_email_key' present

test_cases.test_name: Edge case: Re-running migration is idempotent and does not alter schema
preconditions: User schema already migrated once (as in TC-DA-001)
Migration script is idempotent (uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS or similar)
steps: Run migration script a second time: psql -h localhost -U testuser -d testdb -f ./migrations/001_users.sql
Capture any error output
Re-describe users table and verify column definitions unchanged
test_cases.expected_result: Second run completes with exit code 0 and no errors; schema remains identical to first run
test_cases.pass_criteria: Migration exit code = 0 AND stderr empty AND \d users output matches prior run

test_cases.test_name: Failure scenario: Inserting duplicate email violates unique constraint
preconditions: A test user with email 'test@example.com' already inserted
steps: Attempt to insert another user with same email: INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash) VALUES ('test@example.com', 'hash');
Capture SQL error code and message
test_cases.expected_result: Insert fails with PostgreSQL unique_violation error (code 23505)
test_cases.pass_criteria: SQLSTATE = '23505' AND error message contains 'duplicate key value violates unique constraint'

test_cases.test_name: Happy path: Task schema migration runs successfully and creates tables with correct foreign keys and indexes
preconditions: User schema already migrated (users table exists)
steps: Run task migration: psql -h localhost -U testuser -d testdb -f ./migrations/002_tasks.sql
List tables: \dt
Describe tasks table: \d tasks
Check indexes: SELECT indexname FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename = 'tasks';
Verify foreign keys: SELECT conname, confrelid::regclass FROM pg_constraint WHERE conrelid = 'tasks'::regclass AND contype = 'f';
test_cases.expected_result: Migration exit code 0; tasks table includes columns id (serial PK), title, description, owner_id (int), assignee_id (int), status, priority, due_date, created_at, updated_at; foreign keys owner_id -> users(id), assignee_id -> users(id); indexes on owner_id, assignee_id, due_date, and a GIN index on tsvector column for search
test_cases.pass_criteria: Migration exit code = 0 AND table definition matches expected AND foreign keys and indexes present as described

test_cases.test_name: Edge case: Foreign key constraint prevents orphaned task creation
preconditions: No user with id 9999 exists in users table
steps: Attempt to insert a task with owner_id = 9999: INSERT INTO tasks (title, owner_id) VALUES ('Orphan task', 9999);
Capture error
test_cases.expected_result: Insert fails with foreign key violation error (code 23503)
test_cases.pass_criteria: SQLSTATE = '23503' AND error message mentions 'foreign key constraint'

test_cases.test_name: Failure scenario: Deleting a user that has associated tasks is restricted
preconditions: A task owned by user 100 exists
steps: Attempt to delete the user: DELETE FROM users WHERE id = 100;
Capture error
test_cases.expected_result: Delete fails with foreign key violation (code 23503) referencing tasks table
test_cases.pass_criteria: SQLSTATE = '23503' AND error message references 'tasks' table

test_cases.test_name: Happy path: Dashboard query executes under 200ms with realistic data volume
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance with pgbench or similar loaded with 10,000 tasks, 1,000 users, appropriate indexes per task schema
Dashboard query defined (e.g., SELECT t.id, t.title, u.email FROM tasks t JOIN users u ON t.owner_id = u.id WHERE t.due_date >= NOW() ORDER BY t.due_date LIMIT 20);
steps: Run query with EXPLAIN ANALYZE and capture execution time: EXPLAIN ANALYZE <query>;
Repeat 10 times to get average
Ensure no external load
test_cases.expected_result: Average execution time across runs is less than 200 ms

test_cases.test_name: Edge case: Dashboard query remains under 200ms with empty tables
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance with schema migrated but no data in tasks/users tables
steps: Run same dashboard query with EXPLAIN ANALYZE
Capture execution time
test_cases.expected_result: Query executes quickly (typically < 5 ms) well under 200 ms

test_cases.test_name: Failure scenario: Query exceeds 200ms without performance indexes; adding indexes brings it under limit
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance with 100,000 tasks and 10,000 users
Dashboard query as defined, but indexes on owner_id, due_date, and join columns are missing
steps: Run query with EXPLAIN ANALYZE and record time (should be >200ms)
Create missing indexes: CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_owner_id ON tasks(owner_id); CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_due_date ON tasks(due_date);
Run query again with EXPLAIN ANALYZE and record time
test_cases.expected_result: First run exceeds 200 ms; second run after indexes is under 200 ms
test_cases.pass_criteria: First execution_time > 200 ms AND second execution_time < 200 ms
